japanese_name: &honey ディープモイスト ヘアオイル 3.0
description: &honey hair oil. Glossy, sweet-smelling damage control in the gold bottle. Grab it at a drugstore when hotel shampoo leaves your hair emotionally unavailable.

japanese_name: 10円パン
description: 10 Yen Cheese Coin Pancake. Giant coin-shaped pancake with stretchy cheese inside. It is the snack version of pulling a cartoon money glitch in Dotonbori.
notes: [AMBIGUOUS: Daiou Cheese 10 Yen Pan, Shin-Okubo 10 Yen Pan stalls, Dotonbori 10 Yen Pan stalls]; No reliable image source found

japanese_name: 551蓬莱 豚まん
description: 551 Horai pork buns. Osaka's warm pork bun flex, fluffy outside and juicy inside. People buy boxes at the station like edible luggage.

japanese_name: セブンカフェ グリーンスムージー
description: 7-Eleven Green Smoothie. Frozen cup, machine-blended in the store, somehow more serious than my breakfast routine. Get it when konbini coffee feels too adult.
notes: [VENUE_CATEGORY: 7-Eleven Japan; Green Smoothie, Strawberry & Banana Soy Smoothie, Berries & Yogurt Smoothie, Acai Banana Smoothie]

japanese_name: TENGA オリジナルバキュームカップ
description: TENGA Original Vacuum Cup. Donki's most awkwardly visible red-and-white souvenir. Adults buy it when bringing home snacks is not deranged enough.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: TENGA Original Vacuum Cup, TENGA Egg, TENGA Spinner, iroha mini]; No reliable image source found

japanese_name: 赤福餅
description: Akafuku mochi. Soft rice cake under smooth red bean paste, straight from the Ise souvenir canon. Buy it early because the shelf life is aggressively short.

japanese_name: 白鶴 生貯蔵酒 180ml
description: Hakutsuru Draft Sake 180ml. Tiny chilled sake bottle, clean and easy to pack. Airport gift shops sell this when your suitcase has exactly one awkward corner left.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: Hakutsuru Draft Sake 180ml, One Cup Ozeki, local jizake mini bottles, airport sake tasting sets]

japanese_name: アルフォートミニチョコレート
description: Alfort. Tiny chocolate biscuits with a little ship on top, because apparently snacks need maritime branding. Grab a box at any konbini for emergency train carbs.

japanese_name: アルフォートミニチョコレート
description: Alfort mini chocolate biscuits. Biscuit on the bottom, chocolate on top, little boat stamped like it has somewhere to be. The konbini desk snack that disappears too fast.

english_name: Allie Chrono Beauty Gel UV EX
japanese_name: アリィー クロノビューティ ジェルUV EX
description: Allie Chrono Beauty Gel UV EX. SPF50+ PA++++ gel sunscreen that dries down cleaner than most beach-bag chaos. Reach for it when Japan humidity tries to turn your face into miso soup.

english_name: Anessa Perfect UV Sunscreen Skincare Milk
japanese_name: アネッサ パーフェクトUV スキンケアミルク NA
description: Anessa Perfect UV Skincare Milk. SPF50+ PA++++ gold-bottle sunscreen with serious sweat resistance. This is the drugstore flex for summer days that feel personally targeted.

english_name: Anessa Perfect UV Sunscreen Skincare Milk
japanese_name: アネッサ パーフェクトUV スキンケアミルク NA
description: Anessa Perfect UV Sunscreen Milk. Gold-bottle SPF50+ PA++++ protection with a thin, milky finish. Buy it before a Japan summer walk turns into a sidewalk hostage situation.

japanese_name: イス脚カバー（アニマル）
description: Animal chair socks. Tiny fabric covers for chair legs so your floor stops screaming. Japan looked at furniture feet and gave them outfits.

japanese_name: Anker Prime Wall Charger (100W, 3 ports, GaN)
description: Anker Prime Wall Charger. 100W, three ports, and small enough to make your old brick look unemployed. The clean travel charger for phones, laptops, and panic charging.

japanese_name: 27gアンパンマンのベビーせんべい
description: Anpanman Baby Snacks. Soft baby rice crackers with Japan's bread-headed hero on the bag. Parents buy these when tiny humans require emergency silence.

japanese_name: 不二家 アンパンマングミ
description: Anpanman gummies. Fruit gummies shaped like the characters, because Japan refuses to let candy be boring. Kids grab them; adults pretend they are buying them for kids.
notes: Package warns the facility also handles egg, milk, and wheat

input_name: Aomori Apple Products (Juice, Pie, Apple Senbei)
japanese_name: JAアオレン 青森りんごジュース
description: JA Aoren Aomori apple juice. Straightforward apple juice from Japan's apple capital, no mascot required. Buy it at Aomori shops when you want the fruit without hauling fruit.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: JA Aoren apple juice, Aomori apple pie, apple senbei, dried apple snacks]; No reliable image source found

japanese_name: アパガード プレミオ
description: Apagard Premio Toothpaste. Nano-hydroxyapatite toothpaste, fluoride-free, and very Japanese-drugstore coded. People buy it when regular toothpaste feels too basic.

japanese_name: よーじや あぶらとり紙
description: Yojiya aburatorigami. Kyoto oil-blotting papers that pull shine off your face without wrecking makeup. Keep them in your bag for humid temples, trains, and forehead crimes.
notes: [AMBIGUOUS: Apostroph oil blotting paper, Yojiya aburatorigami]

japanese_name: アクエリアス
description: Aquarius. Japan's other default sports drink, lighter and sweeter than Pocari. Vending machines have it ready for post-onsen dehydration and questionable summer decisions.

japanese_name: ご当地キットカット
description: Regional KitKat packs. Japan turns every area into a chocolate wafer flavor and somehow keeps getting away with it. Airport shops are where suitcase space goes to die.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: Hokkaido Azuki & Strawberry, Tokyo Shima Lemon, Kyoto Uji Matcha, Osaka Castle Sakura]

japanese_name: 残波 ホワイト 泡盛
description: Zanpa White Awamori. Okinawan distilled spirit with a cleaner bite than sake. Buy it when your souvenir plan says beach uncle, not tiny cookie box.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: Zanpa White, Ryukyu 1429, Kumesen, Zuisen]; No reliable image source found

japanese_name: おむつが臭わない袋 BOS ベビー用
description: BOS odor-proof diaper bags. Tiny bags that lock in diaper smell like they signed an NDA. Parents buy these before long train rides and public trash-bin anxiety.

japanese_name: サントリー BOSS レインボーマウンテンブレンド
description: BOSS canned coffee. Sweet milk coffee in a tiny can, built for vending machines and salaryman pacing. Drink it when jet lag starts managing your itinerary.

japanese_name: ベビーフット イージーパック
description: Baby Foot Peel Mask. Plastic booties that make your feet shed like a molting lizard. Use it after walking 30,000 steps across Tokyo and questioning your life choices.
